intro: Pouteria spuria, also known as Guanacaste or Cebil, is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria spuria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pouteria spuria is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and extracts, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
